title: Seeing Evangelion Q 30 in Japan
wikitext:
[]: Seeing Evangelion: Q/3.0 in Japan             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Fri Aug 10, 2012 9:57 am Thought it may be good to get some discussion going amongst any users who may be seeing the movie when it drops in Japan this November. I'll actually be over there 19-26 November and going to take the opportunity to see it - Even though I won't understand a single bit of it because my Japanese sucks, haha. Here's the list of cinemas screening Q/3.0: http://ticket.evangelion.co.jp/cinemalist.html#tokyo The Google page translated version: http://goo.gl/MyAHQ I might hit up the Toho complex in Kinshichō, as I always usually stay in Asakusabashi whenever I'm in Tokyo (Nice and close to Akiba). Don't know exactly which day/night I'll go yet but once I know, I'll book my ticket online. So... Is anyone else doing this perchance? Come on, out of the woodwork with you!

[]:             Quote Post by Justacrazyguy » Fri Aug 10, 2012 9:59 am Unless I become a wizard that will be impossible for me. Blue Monday , you are a lucky guy!(even if your Japanese sucks)

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Aug 10, 2012 1:50 pm A friend of a friend is going to be seeing it when it comes out in Japan. Other than that, I've been praying for word of a magical theater here in the States that would screen Q with subtitles with only a few days' delay on the Japanese release. Would be nice, right? I've been jokingly asking my friend when we're going to be booking our tickets to Japan ever since we saw 1.0 and 2.0 last summer.

[]:             Quote Post by StratoSakuya...AF » Fri Aug 10, 2012 10:08 pm I'm already making reservations, but I'm not sure about understanding it, spoken Japanese is always easier in real life.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Sun Aug 12, 2012 7:32 pm I don't think I will be able to swing something like that. I do hope I will be able to for FINAL though.

[]:             Quote Post by JoeD80 » Mon Aug 13, 2012 4:18 pm If I get my passport renewed in time I will go!

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Aug 22, 2012 8:52 am I don't care who goes, but I do hope that the person which will be going is going to record it and put it online for everyone to see. I am saying this without a qualm. I would love to pay for it, but if they let us wait for another year just because we are in the west, I just need to pirate it. off:

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Aug 22, 2012 8:55 am robersora wrote: I don't care who goes, but I do hope that the person which will be going is going to record it and put it online for everyone to see.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Wed Aug 22, 2012 9:46 am Some Japanese fan (s) could do make the cam for the rest of the world.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Aug 22, 2012 9:58 am AR-99 wrote: Some Japanese fan (s) could do make the cam for the rest of the world.

[]:             Quote Post by Murmur » Wed Aug 22, 2012 10:08 am In the meanwhile, I'll use "us" to refer to everyone unable to go to Japan see that movie. We're going to be real busy here, sharpening our tools, preparing the torture room, and we'll be ready to "greet" you once you're back, "asking" you how it was, and what happens with Asuka. And everyone else too, but mostly Asuka.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Wed Aug 22, 2012 10:10 am Evangelion 1.0 Theater Release: September 1, 2007 Evangelion 1.01 DVD Release: April 25, 2008 Evangelion 2.0 Theater Release: June 27, 2009 Evangelion 2.22 DVD/BR Release: May 26, 2010 Evangelion 3.0 Theater Release: November 17, 2012 If the trend continues with 3.0, we'll have to wait about 8-11 months before it's released to home media in Japan, which means it'll be about a year before decent quality fansubs start popping up. So unless they plan on staying off of the internet for a year to avoid spoilers, anyone outside of Japan will have to watch a camrip.... EDIT: However, since FINAL is supposed to come out in 2013, maybe they'll anticipate the home media release of 3.0 by a few months compared to the other two! ...Or maybe I'm expecting to much of Director Anno.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Aug 22, 2012 10:11 am Murmur wrote: In the meanwhile, I'll use "us" to refer to everyone unable to go to Japan see that movie.

[]:             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Wed Aug 22, 2012 11:12 am riffraff11235 wrote: If the trend continues with 3.0, we'll have to wait about 8-11 months before it's released to home media in Japan, which means it'll be about a year before decent quality fansubs start popping up. So unless they plan on staying off of the internet for a year to avoid spoilers, anyone outside of Japan will have to watch a camrip.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Aug 22, 2012 12:40 pm TheFriskyIan wrote: Even then, it will be suite some time before it gets licensed here in the U.S., so it will be a while before it comes out on the big screen here, and then get a DVD/BD release here.

[]:             Quote Post by Murmur » Wed Aug 22, 2012 2:05 pm Hyper Shinchan wrote: That's where piracy becomes handy

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Wed Aug 22, 2012 2:11 pm Murmur wrote: That apart, is there any rule against spoilers on this forum, or should I avoid coming here once the movie is out?

[]:             Quote Post by Chroma » Wed Aug 22, 2012 5:26 pm riffraff11235 wrote: EDIT: However, since FINAL is supposed to come out in 2013, maybe they'll anticipate the home media release of 3.0 by a few months compared to the other two! ...Or maybe I'm expecting to much of Director Anno.

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Wed Aug 22, 2012 10:54 pm I don't know anything about getting camrips (I never saw the first two movies until I bought the US release) so I will be screwed until the home video release. I just hope that when 3.0 is released on Blu-ray, there will be a simlutanious release a la Gundam Unicorn.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Thu Aug 23, 2012 3:01 am Chroma wrote: Let's hope that dear director Anno decides that this is a good idea.
